question: When will the front-month WTI crude oil price return to our defined normalization threshold (WTI ≤ $68.26/bbl)?
event_definition: WTI crude oil price falls_below threshold $68.26/bbl
key_assumptions: WTI crude oil follows a jump-diffusion process (drift μ, vol σ, jump prob ~0.075/day)
Jump sizes ~ Normal(μ_J, σ_J); at most 1 jump per day (standard small-dt approximation)
Threshold τ = $68.26/bbl (2025 75th percentile (user-specified normalization threshold)); event fires when WTI ≤ τ
2020-04-20 negative price anomaly excluded from model fitting
Trading-day based simulation; ~252 trading days per year
key_uncertainties: Jump component identifiability - few large discrete shocks in data vs many small moves
Regime stationarity - will 19-year jump-diffusion parameters hold over forecast horizon?
EVT cross-check: EVT vs MC difference >10pp
Oil market structural changes (energy transition, geopolitics) not in historical data
